meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Learn a professional client-focused workflow to build your first AI project using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start now.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is solved by switching from 'what can I build' to 'what problem can I solve for a user.' By adopting a professional client workflow, you simulate real-world constraints. This transforms abstract coding exercises into concrete, portfolio-ready products that demonstrate genuine value to future employers.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor on an empty IDE is the graveyard of ambition. You have the technical skills, the stack, and the desire, but you are paralyzed by the 'blank page' syndrome. Most students fail here because they treat coding as a solitary academic exercise rather than a service-oriented craft.

To break this cycle, we shift the paradigm: stop being a coder and start being a solution architect. We will use a professional client workflow to extract requirements, define success metrics, and ship an AI-powered MVP. By leveraging Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will turn your first project into a career-defining asset.

steps.title: Define the Client Persona
steps.description: Identify a specific niche—like a local bakery or a student club—and document their biggest recurring manual task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product manager. Generate 3 specific, low-complexity AI-driven project ideas for a local small business that can be built in a weekend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a list of 5 user stories for a small business inventory tracker app.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how data flows. This prevents 'spaghetti code' early in your journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean JSON schema for a SaaS MVP that tracks client invoices and includes a simple AI-summarized status report.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.prisma file based on the client invoice tracking requirements.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Logic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate your boilerplate, ensuring best practices like error handling are baked in from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Python FastAPI backend with modular routing and integrated logging for an AI task-automation tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure and install necessary dependencies for a FastAPI backend.'

steps.title: Implement AI Integration
steps.description: Connect your app to an LLM provider using Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of API orchestration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust service layer that sends user input to an LLM and handles rate limiting and response parsing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Write a service function in Python that calls the Claude API with error handling for network timeouts.'

steps.title: Stress Test and Refine
steps.description: Simulate client feedback loops to iterate on your MVP until it feels polished enough for a portfolio showcase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the core logic of my AI automation tool to ensure stability under load.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the test suite and fix any identified bugs in the service layer.'

pro_tips: Always build for a specific persona; it makes feature prioritization trivial because you can ask 'Would this persona actually use this?'
Treat your Git commit history as a professional diary; future employers value the narrative of your debugging process more than the final code.
Don't optimize for scale on day one; focus on 'Minimum Viable Delight'—make the one core feature work flawlessly.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; documentation is the difference between a project and a product.
Prioritize error states; real-world software is 20% happy path and 80% handling when things go wrong.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a platform like Vercel or Railway, write a one-page case study on why you built it, and link it in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the bridge to your future career. You have the tools, the process, and the vision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
